breaking_changes.title: Model $dates property removed
breaking_changes.description: The $dates property on Eloquent models has been removed in Laravel 9. You must use the $casts property with 'datetime' cast instead. Existing $dates entries must be migrated to $casts.

breaking_changes.title: Custom cast classes implementing Castable must return class string
breaking_changes.description: The Castable interface's castUsing() method now receives an array of $arguments instead of variadic arguments. Classes implementing Castable::castUsing() must update their signature.

migration_example.before: public static function castUsing(...$arguments) { return MyCast::class; }
migration_example.after: public static function castUsing(array $arguments): string { return MyCast::class; }

breaking_changes.title: Model::newCollection() signature changed — array argument required
breaking_changes.description: The newCollection() method on Eloquent models now requires an explicit array type hint on its parameter. Any custom model overriding this method must add the type hint.

migration_example.before: public function newCollection($models = []) { return new CustomCollection($models); }
migration_example.after: public function newCollection(array $models = []): \Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Collection { return new CustomCollection($models); }

breaking_changes.title: AuthenticateWithBasicAuth middleware constructor signature changed
breaking_changes.description: The AuthenticateWithBasicAuth middleware constructor now receives a Guard instance as its first argument rather than resolving it from the container implicitly. Applications extending this middleware must update their constructors.

breaking_changes.title: Gate::after() no longer overrides explicit denials
breaking_changes.description: In Laravel 9, Gate::after() callbacks that return false no longer override an explicit false returned by a Gate::before() or policy. Previously a false from an after() callback could override policy decisions; this was a bug. Applications relying on after() returning false to deny access must migrate to before() or policies.

migration_example.before: Gate::after(function ($user, $ability) { return false; // was incorrectly blocking });
migration_example.after: Gate::before(function ($user, $ability) { return false; // use before() to explicitly deny });

breaking_changes.title: Console Kernel $bootstrappers property removed
breaking_changes.description: The protected $bootstrappers property on Illuminate\Foundation\Console\Kernel has been removed. Applications that overrode $bootstrappers to customise bootstrap classes must switch to overriding the bootstrappers() method.

breaking_changes.title: Schedule::expressionPasses() made protected
breaking_changes.description: The expressionPasses() method on Illuminate\Console\Scheduling\Event was changed from public to protected. External code calling this method directly will throw a fatal error.

migration_example.after: // use isDue() or check via the scheduler — direct access was never part of the public API

breaking_changes.title: ConnectionInterface::select() signature change
breaking_changes.description: The select() method on Illuminate\Database\ConnectionInterface now has an updated signature. Custom connection implementations must add the $useReadPdo parameter to their select() methods.

migration_example.after: public function select(string $query, array $bindings = [], bool $useReadPdo = true): array

breaking_changes.title: Grammar::compileInsertGetId() signature changed
breaking_changes.description: The compileInsertGetId() method in database grammar classes now requires an explicit string type on the $sequence parameter. Custom grammar subclasses must add this type hint.

breaking_changes.title: PDO integer binding (PDO::PARAM_INT) no longer used
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 no longer uses PDO::PARAM_INT when binding integer values to queries. All values are bound as PDO::PARAM_STR. Applications that relied on database-level integer casting from PDO bindings may see behavioural differences with strict comparison in stored procedures or triggers.

migration_example.before: // Laravel internally used PDO::PARAM_INT for integer values
migration_example.after: // All values now bound with PDO::PARAM_STR — ensure stored procs handle string-typed integers

breaking_changes.title: SQLite minimum version bumped to 3.26.0
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 requires SQLite 3.26.0 or higher. Environments running older SQLite versions (common on Ubuntu 18.04 / macOS High Sierra era) will fail at runtime when using SQLite databases.

migration_example.after: # Upgrade SQLite to >= 3.26.0. On Ubuntu: apt-get install sqlite3

breaking_changes.title: Request::route() return type narrowed
breaking_changes.description: The return type of Request::route() is now Route|object|string|null rather than mixed. Code that passes the return value to functions expecting mixed or relies on implicit casting may need updating. Additionally, type-checking the return value is now recommended.

breaking_changes.title: Controller::callAction() signature changed
breaking_changes.description: The callAction() method on Illuminate\Routing\Controller now explicitly type-hints its parameters. Custom controllers or middleware that override callAction() must update their signatures to match.

breaking_changes.title: Symfony HttpFoundation upgraded to v6 — Response::prepare() behaviour changed
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 uses Symfony HttpFoundation 6.x. Response::prepare() now enforces stricter behaviour: Content-Type headers are set more aggressively, and HTTP/2 responses handle headers differently. Applications subclassing Response or manipulating headers directly must be reviewed.

migration_example.before: // Symfony 5 Response::prepare() was lenient about Content-Type on 204/304
migration_example.after: // Review any response subclasses; test all endpoints returning non-200 status codes

breaking_changes.title: Route group namespace option removed
breaking_changes.description: The 'namespace' option in Route::group() and Route::prefix() has been removed. In Laravel 9 you must use fully-qualified class names or import controller classes. The RouteServiceProvider's $namespace property must also be removed or set to null.

breaking_changes.title: Rule::unique()/Rule::exists() whereNot() renamed to whereNotIn()
breaking_changes.description: The whereNot() method on Illuminate\Validation\Rules\Unique and Illuminate\Validation\Rules\Exists has been renamed to whereNotIn(). Call sites using ->whereNot() must be updated.

breaking_changes.title: PHP 8.0 minimum requirement enforced
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 requires PHP 8.0 or higher. PHP 7.x is not supported. The application, all dependencies, and the host runtime must be running PHP 8.0+.

breaking_changes.title: Flysystem upgraded to v3 — Storage facade method changes
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 uses league/flysystem v3. Several Storage facade methods changed: getVisibility()/setVisibility() now use Visibility constants, url() and temporaryUrl() signatures changed, and many methods no longer accept null. The FileExistsException and FileNotFoundException no longer exist.

migration_example.after: Storage::visibility($path); // use visibility() method; returns Visibility::PUBLIC constant

breaking_changes.title: SessionHandlerInterface::write() must return bool
breaking_changes.description: PHP 8.0 changed the SessionHandlerInterface so that the write() method must return bool (not int|bool). Custom session handlers that return 1 (truthy int) or other non-bool values will cause a TypeError at runtime on PHP 8.0+.

migration_example.after: public function write(string $sessionId, string $data): bool { $this->store->put($sessionId, $data); return true; }

breaking_changes.title: Carbon v2 — constructor microsecond handling changed
breaking_changes.description: Carbon v2 (used by Laravel 9) changed how microseconds are handled in the constructor. Passing a datetime string with microseconds may produce unexpected results in some edge cases. Code comparing Carbon instances with microsecond precision should be reviewed.

migration_example.before: $dt = new Carbon('2021-01-01 12:00:00.000000'); // microseconds may be stripped

breaking_changes.title: SwiftMailer replaced by Symfony Mailer
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 replaces SwiftMailer with Symfony Mailer. The Swift_Message class and all Swift_* classes are gone. Mailables, transporters, and plugins relying on SwiftMailer must be migrated. The Message class now wraps Symfony's Email object. Custom mail plugins implementing Swift_Events_SendListener must be rewritten.

migration_example.after: // Use Mailable classes with attachments() method or $mail->html() on Symfony Email

breaking_changes.title: Str::slug() now uses ASCII transliteration by default
breaking_changes.description: Str::slug() and Str::ascii() now use a different underlying transliteration library in Laravel 9 (via voku/portable-ascii). Some characters that previously passed through may now be transliterated or stripped differently.

migration_example.before: Str::slug('Héllo Wörld'); // may produce slightly different results
migration_example.after: // Test slug output for non-ASCII characters after upgrade; review URL generation

breaking_changes.title: CastsAttributes interface now requires return type declarations
breaking_changes.description: The CastsAttributes interface in Laravel 9 has updated signatures for get() and set(). Custom cast classes that implement CastsAttributes must update their method signatures to include nullable return types.

migration_example.before: public function get($model, string $key, $value, array $attributes) { return ...; }
migration_example.after: public function get(mixed $model, string $key, mixed $value, array $attributes): mixed { return ...; }

breaking_changes.title: HTTP Client throw() method added — exception handling changed
breaking_changes.description: The HTTP Client's throw() method now accepts a closure. Existing code checking response status with ->throw() should be reviewed to ensure the new closure-based API doesn't conflict with existing exception handling.

breaking_changes.title: Queue ShouldQueue jobs — handle() return type must be void
breaking_changes.description: In Laravel 9, the handle() method on queued jobs should declare a void return type. While PHP does not enforce this for interface compliance in all cases, strict analysis tools and future Laravel versions require it.

breaking_changes.title: API middleware throttle string changed from 'throttle:6,1' to 'throttle:api'
breaking_changes.description: In Laravel 9, the default API rate limiter is defined via RouteServiceProvider::configureRateLimiting(). The api middleware group should reference 'throttle:api' (a named rate limiter) instead of the legacy 'throttle:6,1' format.

breaking_changes.title: Model::unguard() and Model::reguard() static calls removed
breaking_changes.description: The Model::unguard() and Model::reguard() static calls were commonly used in seeders and tests to temporarily disable mass-assignment protection. In Laravel 9+, the recommended approach is to set protected $guarded = [] on individual models or use forceFill(). These calls are removed automatically.

breaking_changes.title: Password validation rule helper methods renamed
breaking_changes.description: The helper methods on Illuminate\Validation\Rules\Password were renamed in Laravel 9: requireLetters() → letters(), requireMixedCase() → mixedCase(), requireNumbers() → numbers(), requireSymbols() → symbols(), requireUncompromised() → uncompromised().

breaking_changes.title: Eloquent accessor/mutator style — new Attribute::make() syntax available
breaking_changes.description: Laravel 9 introduced a simplified attribute accessor/mutator syntax using Attribute::make(). The old getFooAttribute()/setFooAttribute() style still works but is deprecated. The new syntax consolidates getter and setter into a single method returning an Attribute object.

migration_example.after: public function firstName(): Attribute { return Attribute::make(get: fn ($value) => ucfirst($value)); }
